sourceMovie.title: Departures
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Departures (2008) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Departures clicked for you, Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: If I Stay
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Departures clicked for you, If I Stay (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: High School Musical 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Departures clicked for you, High School Musical 2 (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Departures?
faq.answer: Try Sweeney Todd: The Demon Barber of Fleet Street, If I Stay, High School Musical 2—each shares DNA with Departures while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Departures?
faq.answer: Departures (2008) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Departures worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
